behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is request-shaping logic inside the classifier before I/O. A unit test can call request-builder/classify with a stub client and assert the payload contains every taxonomy label. Rejected Integration because behavior text is about payload content, not cross-module state handoff; rejected System/Agentic because no real network or model judgment is required.
behaviors.test_description: Construct classifier with taxonomy [`legal`,`technical`,`financial`,`other`] and a capturing fake client. Invoke classification once and assert outgoing request includes the full taxonomy list exactly once and untruncated.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic normalization/validation logic on returned label values. A stubbed API response with an out-of-taxonomy label is sufficient to expose real failure. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require multi-module state propagation; rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model quality, only post-processing rule.
behaviors.test_description: Stub API response label as `medical` while taxonomy excludes it; assert module output is `unknown` and never `medical`.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly isolates normalization determinism for identical inputs, which is a pure local transformation. Rejected higher levels because text disqualifies infrastructure dependence: 'identical raw API responses' means no need for live API/network/model variability.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the same raw response payload twice (or many times) into normalization path and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real model behavior across repeated live calls; stubs cannot reveal non-deterministic label drift. Behavior text explicitly requires 'repeated live API calls' and consistency contingent on provider response changes, which is model-output dependent. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real calls for same document/taxonomy, record provider raw outputs and final labels, and assert label changes only when provider output content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at module boundary: token-budget policy + request builder + dispatch gate must coordinate so over-budget content is truncated/rejected before send. Unit on a single helper risks missing handoff bugs (e.g., policy computed but not enforced before client call). Rejected System because behavior says 'before dispatch' and can be validated with in-process fake client; rejected Agentic because no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with real budget-enforcer/request-builder modules and fake transport. Provide oversized document and assert configured policy is applied (truncate or reject) and transport sees only compliant payload (or no call on reject).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Needs real external API/token accounting to catch mismatches between local expectations and provider-reported usage; mocks can hide this. Rejected Agentic because assertion is about usage metering, not semantic quality/classification correctness. Rejected Integration due REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly depends on provider-reported values from live call.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification call with representative document, read provider usage metadata, and assert reported prompt/total tokens are <= configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is retry orchestration across modules (API client wrapper, backoff/wait logic, and caller-facing error handling). In-process fake API and controllable clock can reproduce 429-then-success sequence and catch real handoff bugs. Rejected System because behavior does not require real network timing; rejected Agentic because no model-output-content dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake transport to return rate-limit response first, success second; use fake clock/sleeper to assert wait occurred and retry happened, and caller receives success instead of immediate error.
